habitat: Primary dipterocarp forest; peatswamp forest; lowland rainforests
story: Discover the stunning Bornean Bristlehead (*Pityriasis gymnocephala*), a member of the diverse Pityriasidae family. Native to Primary dipterocarp forest; peatswamp forest; lowland rainforests, this bird faces various environmental challenges. Currently classified as Near Threatened (NT) on the IUCN Red List, the Bornean Bristlehead represents the delicate balance of its native region. Understanding its habitat requirements and distribution is key to effective conservation planning. This species contributes to the richness of avian diversity, and this poster offers an artistic glimpse into its world.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
